entity.name: Atrial Fibrillation
entity.description: Most common sustained arrhythmia; thrombus typically forms in the left atrial appendage, the target of LAA closure for stroke prevention when anticoagulation is unsuitable.

entity.name: Patent Foramen Ovale (PFO-associated stroke)
entity.description: Residual interatrial communication implicated in paradoxical embolism and cryptogenic stroke; transcatheter closure reduces recurrent stroke in selected patients.

entity.name: Atrial Septal Defect
entity.description: Congenital opening in the atrial septum causing left-to-right shunt; secundum defects are amenable to transcatheter closure.

entity.name: Ventricular Septal Defect
entity.description: Congenital or post-infarction defect in the ventricular septum; selected muscular and post-MI defects are treated with transcatheter occluders.

entity.name: Patent Ductus Arteriosus
entity.description: Persistent fetal connection between aorta and pulmonary artery; transcatheter closure is standard, including in premature infants.

entity.name: Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
entity.description: Sarcomeric cardiomyopathy with LV hypertrophy; the obstructive form (HOCM) features dynamic LVOT obstruction treated by septal reduction or cardiac myosin inhibitors.

entity.name: Transthyretin Amyloid Cardiomyopathy
entity.description: Infiltrative cardiomyopathy from misfolded transthyretin (wild-type or hereditary) depositing in the myocardium; treated with TTR stabilizers and silencers.

data.label: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction

entity.name: Heart Failure with Reduced Ejection Fraction
entity.description: Heart failure with LVEF <=40%; managed with guideline-directed medical therapy (four pillars) and increasingly with hemodynamic monitoring.

data.label: Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction

entity.name: Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction
entity.description: Heart failure with LVEF >=50% and elevated filling pressures; emerging therapies include SGLT2 inhibitors, finerenone, and interatrial shunts.

entity.name: Coronary Microvascular Dysfunction & Refractory Angina
entity.description: Angina from impaired microvascular function (often with non-obstructive coronaries); device approaches include coronary sinus narrowing to redistribute perfusion.

entity.name: Aortic Stenosis
entity.description: Progressive narrowing of the aortic valve (commonly calcific/degenerative); severe symptomatic disease is treated by TAVR or surgical AVR, with trials now extending TAVR to low-risk and asymptomatic patients.

entity.name: Aortic Regurgitation
entity.description: Diastolic backflow through an incompetent aortic valve causing LV volume overload; historically surgical, now with the first dedicated transcatheter device (JenaValve Trilogy) for symptomatic severe AR.

entity.name: Mitral Regurgitation
entity.description: Systolic backflow through the mitral valve, either primary/degenerative (leaflet/chordal) or secondary/functional (annular dilation, LV remodeling); treated by transcatheter edge-to-edge repair (TEER), transcatheter replacement (TMVR), annuloplasty, or surgery.

entity.name: Mitral Stenosis
entity.description: Obstruction to LA-to-LV inflow, most often rheumatic (commissural fusion) or degenerative with mitral annular calcification; treated by percutaneous balloon commissurotomy or surgery.

entity.name: Tricuspid Regurgitation
entity.description: Backflow through the tricuspid valve, frequently functional from annular dilation/right-heart remodeling; long undertreated, now addressed by transcatheter edge-to-edge repair (TriClip) and transcatheter replacement (EVOQUE).

entity.name: Pulmonary Valve Disease
entity.description: Pulmonary regurgitation/stenosis or RVOT conduit dysfunction, often in repaired congenital heart disease (e.g. tetralogy of Fallot); treated by transcatheter pulmonary valve replacement (Melody, Harmony) or surgery.

entity.name: Watchman FLX
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved (Watchman 2015; FLX 2020; FLX Pro 2023).
entity.mechanism: Percutaneously seals the left atrial appendage to prevent thrombus embolization in non-valvular AF.

entity.name: Amplatzer Amulet
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2021; dual-seal (lobe + disc) design.
entity.mechanism: Dual-seal LAA occluder for stroke prevention in non-valvular AF.

entity.name: LAmbre
entity.regulatoryDetail: CE mark and China NMPA approved; investigational in the US.

timeline.notes: Year-level CE mark date; refine if an exact notified-body date is verified.

entity.mechanism: Umbrella-and-cover LAA occluder designed for variable appendage anatomy.

entity.name: Amplatzer PFO Occluder
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2016 for recurrent cryptogenic stroke due to PFO.

timeline.event: FDA approval for recurrent cryptogenic stroke due to PFO

entity.mechanism: Double-disc nitinol occluder that closes the patent foramen ovale.

entity.name: GORE Cardioform Septal Occluder
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved for PFO and for secundum ASD closure.
entity.mechanism: ePTFE-covered nitinol frame occluder for PFO and atrial septal defects.

entity.name: Amplatzer Septal Occluder (ASO)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Long-established transcatheter device for secundum ASD.

timeline.event: FDA approval for secundum ASD closure

entity.mechanism: Self-expanding double-disc occluder for secundum atrial septal defects.

entity.name: Amplatzer Piccolo Occluder
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2019; usable in premature infants >=700 g.
entity.mechanism: Miniature occluder for transcatheter closure of patent ductus arteriosus.

entity.name: Amplatzer Muscular VSD Occluder
entity.regulatoryDetail: Approved for muscular VSDs; membranous-VSD use remains investigational/off-label.

timeline.event: FDA humanitarian device exemption for muscular VSD closure

entity.mechanism: Double-disc occluder for muscular ventricular septal defects.

entity.name: Corvia Atrial Shunt
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational; FDA Breakthrough Device. Responder-subgroup signal in REDUCE LAP-HF II.
entity.mechanism: Implanted interatrial shunt that decompresses the left atrium to lower exertional filling pressure in HFpEF.

entity.name: Ventura Interatrial Shunt
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational. RELIEVE-HF neutral overall with a divergent HFrEF-subgroup signal.
entity.mechanism: Interatrial shunt to reduce left atrial pressure across the HF spectrum.

entity.name: APTURE Transcatheter Shunt
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational shunt-to-coronary-sinus system under clinical study (ALT FLOW).
entity.mechanism: Creates a left atrium-to-coronary-sinus shunt to lower left atrial pressure.

entity.name: CardioMEMS HF System
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved; indication expanded to NYHA II-III HF (CHAMPION, GUIDE-HF).
entity.mechanism: Wireless pulmonary-artery pressure sensor enabling pressure-guided HF management.

entity.name: Cordella PA Sensor
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2024 (PROACTIVE-HF); now part of Edwards Lifesciences.
entity.mechanism: Pulmonary-artery pressure sensor with a paired patient-management platform.

entity.name: FIRE1 NORM System
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational inferior vena cava sensor for congestion monitoring (FUTURE-HF).
entity.mechanism: Implantable IVC sensor that tracks vessel-area changes as a congestion surrogate.

entity.name: V-LAP
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational wireless left-atrial pressure sensor (VECTOR-HF).
entity.mechanism: Direct left-atrial pressure measurement for granular pressure-guided HF management.

entity.name: Coronary Sinus Reducer
entity.regulatoryDetail: CE marked in Europe; investigational in the US (COSIRA-II).
entity.mechanism: Hourglass stent narrows the coronary sinus to raise backward pressure and redistribute perfusion to ischemic myocardium.

entity.name: A-Flux (CMD device)
entity.mechanism: User-flagged device targeting coronary microvascular dysfunction.

curation.notes: Placeholder from user request ('A-flux for CMD'). VERIFY exact product name and manufacturer before curating; add company ref and sources.

entity.name: Mavacamten (Camzyos)
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2022 for symptomatic obstructive HCM; REMS program.

timeline.event: FDA approval for symptomatic obstructive HCM

entity.mechanism: Reversibly inhibits cardiac myosin ATPase, reducing hypercontractility and LVOT obstruction.

entity.name: Aficamten
entity.regulatoryDetail: Next-generation myosin inhibitor; FDA review pending after SEQUOIA-HCM. VERIFY current approval status.
entity.mechanism: Selective cardiac myosin inhibitor reducing LVOT gradients with a wider therapeutic window.

curation.notes: Regulatory status time-sensitive; confirm at next refresh.

entity.name: Tafamidis (Vyndaqel/Vyndamax)
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2019 for ATTR-CM (ATTR-ACT).
entity.mechanism: Stabilizes the transthyretin tetramer, slowing amyloid fibril formation.

entity.name: Acoramidis (Attruby)
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2024 for ATTR-CM (ATTRibute-CM).
entity.mechanism: Near-complete TTR tetramer stabilizer mimicking a protective TTR variant.

entity.name: Vutrisiran (Amvuttra)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Approved for hATTR polyneuropathy; ATTR-CM indication via HELIOS-B (2025). VERIFY label.

timeline.notes: Existing curated record asks to verify the label; keep under human review.

entity.mechanism: Subcutaneous RNAi that lowers hepatic transthyretin production.

entity.name: Patisiran (Onpattro)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Approved for hATTR polyneuropathy; ATTR-CM indication NOT approved (APOLLO-B; FDA declined 2023).
entity.mechanism: IV RNAi that lowers transthyretin production.

entity.name: Dapagliflozin (Farxiga)
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved across the HF spectrum (DAPA-HF, DELIVER).
entity.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibition with pleiotropic cardiorenal benefit; a pillar of GDMT.

entity.name: Empagliflozin (Jardiance)
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved across the HF spectrum (EMPEROR-Reduced/Preserved); co-marketed with Eli Lilly.
entity.mechanism: SGLT2 inhibitor reducing HF hospitalization across ejection fractions.

entity.name: Sacubitril/Valsartan (Entresto)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Approved for chronic HF; greatest benefit at lower EF (PARADIGM-HF; PARAGON-HF).

timeline.event: FDA approval for chronic heart failure

entity.mechanism: Angiotensin receptor-neprilysin inhibitor augmenting natriuretic peptides while blocking RAAS.

entity.name: Vericiguat (Verquvo)
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2021 for worsening HFrEF (VICTORIA).
entity.mechanism: Soluble guanylate cyclase stimulator restoring the NO-sGC-cGMP pathway.

entity.name: Finerenone (Kerendia)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Approved for CKD in T2D; HFmrEF/HFpEF benefit shown in FINEARTS-HF (2024). VERIFY HF label.

timeline.event: FDA approval for CKD in T2D
timeline.notes: HF label remains flagged for verification in the curated record.

entity.mechanism: Nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist reducing cardiorenal inflammation/fibrosis.

entity.name: Omecamtiv Mecarbil
entity.regulatoryDetail: GALACTIC-HF modestly positive; FDA complete response letter (2023) - not approved.
entity.mechanism: Selective cardiac myosin activator increasing systolic ejection time and stroke volume.

entity.name: Surgical Septal Myectomy
entity.regulatoryDetail: Established surgical standard of care for obstructive HCM at experienced centers.
entity.mechanism: Surgical resection of hypertrophied basal septum to relieve LVOT obstruction.

entity.name: Alcohol Septal Ablation
entity.regulatoryDetail: Established percutaneous alternative to myectomy in selected patients.
entity.mechanism: Catheter-delivered ethanol infarcts a septal perforator to thin the basal septum and reduce obstruction.

entity.name: Story Health Platform
entity.regulatoryDetail: Care-delivery software (not an FDA-cleared DTx); classification varies. VERIFY.
entity.mechanism: AI-assisted remote titration of guideline-directed medical therapy with care-team workflows.

curation.notes: Confirm structural-heart relevance, indication, and regulatory classification.

entity.name: Hello Heart
entity.regulatoryDetail: Consumer digital health app for BP/CV risk; not a structural-heart-specific therapy. VERIFY relevance.
entity.mechanism: App-based blood-pressure tracking and behavioral coaching for cardiovascular risk.

curation.notes: Indication mapping is loose; refine or remove during curation.

entity.name: Edwards SAPIEN 3 / SAPIEN 3 Ultra RESILIA
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved across surgical risk strata (high/inoperable 2015 lineage; intermediate 2016; low-risk 2019). Also used for aortic/mitral/tricuspid/pulmonary valve-in-valve.

timeline.event: FDA approval of SAPIEN 3 lineage for high/inoperable-risk aortic stenosis

entity.mechanism: Balloon-expandable bovine-pericardial valve on a cobalt-chromium frame, deployed within the native aortic annulus.

entity.name: Medtronic Evolut FX / FX+
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved across risk strata (high 2014 CoreValve lineage; intermediate 2017; low-risk 2019); supra-annular design favored in small annuli.

timeline.event: FDA approval of CoreValve/Evolut lineage for high-risk aortic stenosis

entity.mechanism: Self-expanding supra-annular porcine-pericardial valve on a nitinol frame for the native aortic annulus.

entity.name: Abbott Navitor
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2023 (CE mark 2021); intra-annular sealing cuff (NaviSeal) to reduce paravalvular leak.
entity.mechanism: Self-expanding bovine-pericardial valve with an active sealing cuff for the native aortic annulus.

entity.name: Boston Scientific ACURATE neo2
entity.regulatoryDetail: CE-marked self-expanding TAVR; Boston Scientific wound down the ACURATE platform in 2025 after ACURATE IDE failed noninferiority vs SAPIEN/Evolut. Not FDA approved.

timeline.notes: Year-level CE mark date; platform later discontinued.

entity.mechanism: Self-expanding porcine-pericardial valve with a top-down deployment and stabilization arches.

entity.name: JenaValve Trilogy
entity.regulatoryDetail: First transcatheter heart valve approved for symptomatic severe aortic regurgitation (FDA 2025; CE mark for AR and AS). VERIFY exact approval dates.

timeline.event: FDA approval for symptomatic severe aortic regurgitation
timeline.notes: Existing curated record asks to verify exact approval dates.

entity.mechanism: Self-expanding valve with anatomically-aligned locators that clip the native leaflets, enabling fixation without annular calcium (key for pure AR).

entity.name: Abbott Tendyne
entity.regulatoryDetail: CE mark 2020 for mitral regurgitation; US investigational (SUMMIT pivotal trial). Transapical delivery with an apical tether.
entity.mechanism: Self-expanding porcine-pericardial mitral valve anchored by an apical pad via a tether; fully repositionable/retrievable.

entity.name: Edwards SAPIEN M3
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational transseptal TMVR (ENCIRCLE pivotal trial); uses a dock to capture a SAPIEN valve in the mitral position.
entity.mechanism: Nitinol dock encircles the mitral leaflets/chordae, then a balloon-expandable SAPIEN valve is implanted within the dock.

curation.notes: One-year ENCIRCLE-MAC Registry data presented at New York Valves 2026 (Jun 24-26). SAPIEN M3 reportedly received FDA approval in late 2025 as the first transseptal TMVR; human curator: VERIFY the approval and, if confirmed, update regulatoryStatus to approved with regulatoryDetail and a fda-approval timeline.

entity.name: Medtronic Intrepid
entity.regulatoryDetail: Investigational TMVR (APOLLO pivotal trial); transseptal delivery system in development.
entity.mechanism: Self-expanding dual-stent bovine-pericardial mitral valve with a conformable outer fixation ring.

entity.name: Abbott MitraClip
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved for primary/degenerative MR (2013) and for secondary/functional MR in heart failure (2019, on COAPT); now in G4 generation.

timeline.event: FDA approval for primary/degenerative mitral regurgitation

entity.mechanism: Percutaneous transseptal clip that grasps the mitral leaflets to create a double-orifice valve and reduce regurgitation.

entity.name: Edwards PASCAL / PASCAL Precision
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved for degenerative MR 2022 (CLASP IID); central spacer plus independently-graspable paddles.

timeline.event: FDA approval for degenerative mitral regurgitation

entity.mechanism: Transseptal edge-to-edge repair device with a central spacer that fills the regurgitant orifice and broad paddles for leaflet capture.

curation.notes: New York Valves 2026 (Jun 24-26): real-world PASCAL M-TEER outcomes from >4,500 patients in the STS/ACC TVT Registry presented, reinforcing sustained safety/effectiveness in mitral regurgitation.

entity.name: Abbott TriClip
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved April 2024 for tricuspid regurgitation (on TRILUMINATE Pivotal) — the first transcatheter tricuspid repair device in the US.
entity.mechanism: Transcatheter edge-to-edge clip adapted to tricuspid anatomy to approximate leaflets and reduce TR.

entity.name: Edwards EVOQUE
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved February 2024 (on TRISCEND II) — the first transcatheter tricuspid valve replacement system.
entity.mechanism: Self-expanding bovine-pericardial valve with intra-annular sealing and anchoring tabs that engage native tricuspid leaflets.

entity.name: Edwards Cardioband
entity.regulatoryDetail: CE mark for mitral and tricuspid annular reduction; US investigational. Implanted on the beating heart under imaging guidance.

timeline.event: CE mark for mitral annular reduction
timeline.notes: Earliest known Cardioband CE mark; tricuspid CE mark came later.

entity.mechanism: Adjustable transcatheter annuloplasty band anchored along the valve annulus and cinched to reduce annular dimension and regurgitation.

entity.name: Medtronic Melody TPV
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA Humanitarian Device Exemption 2010; PMA approval 2017. For dysfunctional RVOT conduits/bioprosthetic valves.
entity.mechanism: Balloon-expandable bovine-jugular-vein valve sutured in a platinum-iridium stent for the pulmonary position.

entity.name: Medtronic Harmony TPV
entity.regulatoryDetail: FDA approved 2021 — first TPVR for severe pulmonary regurgitation in a native/surgically-repaired RVOT (e.g. repaired tetralogy of Fallot).
entity.mechanism: Self-expanding porcine-pericardial valve in a nitinol frame designed to seat in the native RVOT/main pulmonary artery.

entity.name: Surgical Aortic Valve Replacement (SAVR)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Standard surgical therapy (mechanical or bioprosthetic valve); the comparator arm in the PARTNER and Evolut TAVR trials.
entity.mechanism: Open surgical excision of the diseased aortic valve and implantation of a mechanical or bioprosthetic prosthesis.

entity.name: Surgical Mitral Valve Repair / Replacement
entity.regulatoryDetail: Standard surgical therapy; repair (annuloplasty + leaflet/chordal techniques) preferred over replacement for degenerative MR.
entity.mechanism: Open or minimally-invasive surgical repair (annuloplasty ring, leaflet resection/neochordae) or prosthetic replacement of the mitral valve.

entity.name: Surgical Tricuspid Valve Repair / Replacement
entity.regulatoryDetail: Standard surgical therapy, classically annuloplasty during left-sided heart surgery; isolated tricuspid surgery carries higher operative risk.
entity.mechanism: Surgical annuloplasty (ring) or prosthetic replacement of the tricuspid valve.

entity.name: Balloon Aortic Valvuloplasty (BAV)
entity.regulatoryDetail: Palliative or bridge therapy; restenosis is common. Also used to facilitate TAVR.
entity.mechanism: Balloon dilation of the stenotic aortic valve to transiently increase valve area.

entity.name: Percutaneous Mitral Balloon Commissurotomy (PMBC)
entity.regulatoryDetail: First-line therapy for suitable rheumatic mitral stenosis (favorable valve morphology, no significant MR or LA thrombus).
entity.mechanism: Transseptal balloon splitting of fused mitral commissures to enlarge the mitral orifice.

entity.name: Boston Scientific
entity.description: Medical device maker; LAA closure (Watchman franchise).

entity.name: Abbott
entity.description: Structural heart portfolio incl. Amplatzer occluders, Amulet, and CardioMEMS.

entity.name: W. L. Gore & Associates
entity.description: Maker of GORE Cardioform septal/PFO occluders.

entity.name: Lifetech Scientific
entity.description: Structural heart occluders incl. the LAmbre LAA closure device.

entity.name: Corvia Medical
entity.description: Developer of the Corvia Atrial Shunt for heart failure.

entity.name: V-Wave
entity.description: Interatrial shunt (Ventura) developer; acquired by Johnson & Johnson MedTech (2024).

entity.name: Edwards Lifesciences
entity.description: Structural heart company; APTURE transcatheter shunt and (via Endotronix) the Cordella sensor.

entity.name: Endotronix
entity.description: Cordella PA pressure sensor; acquired by Edwards Lifesciences (2024).

entity.name: FIRE1
entity.description: Foundry-backed developer of the NORM inferior vena cava (IVC) sensor for HF monitoring.

entity.name: Vectorious Medical Technologies
entity.description: Developer of the V-LAP wireless left-atrial pressure sensor.

entity.name: Shockwave Medical (Johnson & Johnson)
entity.description: Coronary Sinus Reducer (via Neovasc acquisition); Shockwave acquired by J&J (2024).

curation.notes: Reducer lineage: Neovasc -> Shockwave (2023) -> J&J (2024). Verify.

entity.name: Bristol Myers Squibb
entity.description: Markets mavacamten (Camzyos) for HCM, via the MyoKardia acquisition.

entity.name: Cytokinetics
entity.description: Muscle-biology company; aficamten (HCM) and omecamtiv mecarbil (HFrEF).

entity.name: Pfizer
entity.description: Markets tafamidis (Vyndaqel/Vyndamax) for ATTR-CM.

entity.name: BridgeBio Pharma
entity.description: Markets acoramidis (Attruby) for ATTR-CM.

entity.name: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals
entity.description: RNAi therapeutics; vutrisiran (Amvuttra) and patisiran (Onpattro) for ATTR.

entity.name: AstraZeneca
entity.description: Markets dapagliflozin (Farxiga/Forxiga), an SGLT2 inhibitor.

entity.name: Boehringer Ingelheim
entity.description: Empagliflozin (Jardiance), an SGLT2 inhibitor co-marketed with Eli Lilly.

entity.name: Novartis
entity.description: Markets sacubitril/valsartan (Entresto), an ARNI.

entity.name: Merck (MSD)
entity.description: Markets vericiguat (Verquvo), an sGC stimulator (co-developed with Bayer).

entity.name: Bayer
entity.description: Finerenone (Kerendia), a nonsteroidal MRA; vericiguat co-developer.

entity.name: Medtronic
entity.description: Structural heart portfolio incl. the Evolut self-expanding TAVR platform, Melody/Harmony transcatheter pulmonary valves, and the investigational Intrepid TMVR.

entity.name: JenaValve Technology
entity.description: Developer of the Trilogy transcatheter heart valve — the first device dedicated to transcatheter treatment of aortic regurgitation.

entity.name: Story Health
entity.description: Digital care platform for guideline-directed HF medical-therapy titration.

curation.notes: Digital health vendor; confirm structural-heart relevance and classification.

entity.name: Hello Heart
entity.description: Digital heart-health app for blood pressure and cardiovascular risk management.

curation.notes: Digital health vendor; refine indication relevance.

entity.name: PROTECT AF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of stroke, cardiovascular death, and systemic embolism
entity.outcomeSummary: Watchman was noninferior (and superior on the long-term composite) to warfarin in non-valvular AF.

entity.name: PREVAIL
entity.primaryEndpoint: Efficacy and safety vs warfarin
entity.outcomeSummary: Co-primary endpoints only partially met, but data supported approval alongside PROTECT AF.

entity.name: PRAGUE-17
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of cardiovascular, neurological, and bleeding events
entity.outcomeSummary: LAA closure was noninferior to DOACs in high-risk AF patients.

entity.name: Amulet IDE
entity.primaryEndpoint: Safety, effective LAA occlusion, and stroke/systemic embolism
entity.outcomeSummary: Amulet was noninferior to Watchman; more peri-procedural complications but superior LAA closure.

entity.name: OPTION
entity.primaryEndpoint: Stroke/death and non-procedural bleeding after AF ablation
entity.outcomeSummary: LAA closure was noninferior for ischemic events with less non-procedural bleeding than DOAC after ablation.

entity.name: CHAMPION-AF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Stroke/systemic embolism and bleeding vs first-line DOAC
entity.outcomeSummary: Large RCT testing first-line LAA closure vs DOAC in a broad AF population; results pending.

curation.notes: Confirm enrollment and readout date at refresh.

entity.name: RESPECT
entity.primaryEndpoint: Recurrent ischemic stroke
entity.outcomeSummary: Extended follow-up showed PFO closure superior to medical therapy for recurrent cryptogenic stroke.

entity.name: Gore REDUCE
entity.primaryEndpoint: Recurrent stroke and new brain infarcts
entity.outcomeSummary: PFO closure reduced recurrent stroke versus antiplatelet therapy alone.

entity.name: CLOSE
entity.primaryEndpoint: Recurrent stroke
entity.outcomeSummary: Closure superior to antiplatelet therapy in patients with high-risk PFO features (multiple devices used).

curation.notes: CLOSE permitted several occluders; therapy ref is representative.

entity.name: EXPLORER-HCM
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite functional/symptomatic improvement (peak VO2 + NYHA)
entity.outcomeSummary: Mavacamten improved exercise capacity, LVOT gradient, symptoms, and quality of life in obstructive HCM.

entity.name: VALOR-HCM
entity.primaryEndpoint: Decision to proceed with septal reduction therapy
entity.outcomeSummary: Mavacamten markedly reduced eligibility/need for septal reduction therapy in severely symptomatic obstructive HCM.

entity.name: SEQUOIA-HCM
entity.primaryEndpoint: Change in peak oxygen uptake (peak VO2)
entity.outcomeSummary: Aficamten significantly improved peak VO2 and all secondary endpoints in obstructive HCM.

entity.name: MAPLE-HCM
entity.primaryEndpoint: Aficamten vs metoprolol as monotherapy (peak VO2)
entity.outcomeSummary: Head-to-head monotherapy comparison against a beta-blocker; ongoing.

entity.name: ATTR-ACT
entity.primaryEndpoint: All-cause mortality and cardiovascular hospitalizations
entity.outcomeSummary: Tafamidis reduced mortality and cardiovascular hospitalizations in ATTR-CM.

entity.name: ATTRibute-CM
entity.primaryEndpoint: Hierarchical composite of death and cardiovascular hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: Acoramidis improved the hierarchical composite endpoint, supporting FDA approval.

entity.name: HELIOS-B
entity.primaryEndpoint: All-cause mortality and recurrent cardiovascular events
entity.outcomeSummary: Vutrisiran reduced mortality and cardiovascular events in ATTR-CM.

entity.name: APOLLO-B
entity.primaryEndpoint: 6-minute walk distance
entity.outcomeSummary: Patisiran improved functional measures, but the FDA declined the ATTR-CM indication.

entity.name: PARADIGM-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: Sacubitril/valsartan was superior to enalapril in HFrEF, establishing ARNI as foundational therapy.

entity.name: DAPA-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Worsening HF or cardiovascular death
entity.outcomeSummary: Dapagliflozin reduced events in HFrEF regardless of diabetes status.

entity.name: DELIVER
entity.primaryEndpoint: Cardiovascular death or worsening HF
entity.outcomeSummary: Dapagliflozin reduced events in HF with mildly reduced or preserved ejection fraction.

entity.name: EMPEROR-Preserved
entity.primaryEndpoint: Cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: First clearly positive HFpEF outcomes trial, driven by reduced HF hospitalizations.

entity.name: EMPEROR-Reduced
entity.primaryEndpoint: Cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: Empagliflozin reduced cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization in HFrEF.

entity.name: VICTORIA
entity.primaryEndpoint: Cardiovascular death or HF hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: Vericiguat reduced events in patients with recently worsening HFrEF.

entity.name: FINEARTS-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Cardiovascular death and total worsening HF events
entity.outcomeSummary: Finerenone reduced worsening-HF events in HF with mildly reduced or preserved ejection fraction.

entity.name: GALACTIC-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of HF event or cardiovascular death
entity.outcomeSummary: Modest reduction in the primary composite; the FDA did not approve omecamtiv mecarbil.

entity.name: REDUCE LAP-HF I
entity.primaryEndpoint: Exercise pulmonary capillary wedge pressure
entity.outcomeSummary: Sham-controlled pilot demonstrated reduced exercise wedge pressure with the atrial shunt.

entity.name: REDUCE LAP-HF II
entity.primaryEndpoint: Hierarchical composite of CV death/stroke, HF events, and KCCQ
entity.outcomeSummary: Neutral overall; a prespecified responder subgroup (without latent pulmonary vascular disease) appeared to benefit.

entity.name: RELIEVE-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: Hierarchical clinical composite (death, transplant/LVAD, HF events, KCCQ)
entity.outcomeSummary: Neutral overall with opposing directional effects: apparent benefit in HFrEF and a harm signal in HFpEF.

entity.name: CHAMPION
entity.primaryEndpoint: HF hospitalizations
entity.outcomeSummary: Pulmonary-artery-pressure-guided management reduced HF hospitalizations.

entity.name: GUIDE-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: All-cause mortality and total HF events
entity.outcomeSummary: Primary endpoint neutral overall; a pre-COVID-19 sensitivity analysis suggested benefit.

entity.name: PROACTIVE-HF
entity.primaryEndpoint: HF events and mortality vs performance goal; safety
entity.outcomeSummary: Met prespecified safety and efficacy goals, supporting FDA approval of the Cordella sensor.

entity.name: COSIRA
entity.primaryEndpoint: Improvement in CCS angina class
entity.outcomeSummary: The coronary sinus Reducer improved angina class in refractory angina versus sham.

entity.name: COSIRA-II
entity.primaryEndpoint: Angina and ischemia endpoints
entity.outcomeSummary: US pivotal sham-controlled trial of the coronary sinus Reducer; ongoing.

entity.name: PARTNER 2A
entity.primaryEndpoint: Death or disabling stroke at 2 years
entity.outcomeSummary: In intermediate-risk severe aortic stenosis, TAVR (SAPIEN XT/3) was noninferior to surgical AVR.

entity.name: PARTNER 3
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or rehospitalization at 1 year
entity.outcomeSummary: In low-surgical-risk severe AS, TAVR with SAPIEN 3 was superior to surgery at 1 year (longer-term curves converge).

curation.notes: New York Valves 2026 (Jun 24-26): 7-year PARTNER 3 valve durability/outcomes presented — SAPIEN 3 durability comparable to surgery in low-risk patients, supporting TAVR in lifetime management. Human curator: consider folding the 7-year readout into outcomeSummary.

entity.name: Evolut Low Risk
entity.primaryEndpoint: Death or disabling stroke at 24 months
entity.outcomeSummary: In low-risk severe AS, self-expanding TAVR (Evolut) was noninferior to surgery.

entity.name: EARLY TAVR
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or unplanned cardiovascular hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: In asymptomatic severe AS, early TAVR reduced events versus clinical surveillance — extending TAVR to asymptomatic disease.

curation.notes: Updated EARLY TAVR efficacy results presented at New York Valves 2026 (Jun 24-26); human curator may refresh outcomeSummary with the longer-term data.

entity.name: PROGRESS
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of death, stroke, and unplanned cardiovascular hospitalization
entity.outcomeSummary: Randomized trial testing TAVR (SAPIEN 3) versus clinical surveillance in moderate, calcific aortic stenosis; baseline clinical and echocardiographic characteristics of the enrolled cohort were presented at New York Valves 2026, with primary outcomes pending.

curation.notes: Baseline characteristics presented at New York Valves 2026 (Jun 24-26); no primary outcome yet (est. completion 2037). VERIFY exact primary endpoint wording from the trial protocol before promoting.

entity.name: SMART
entity.primaryEndpoint: Clinical composite and bioprosthetic valve dysfunction in small annuli
entity.outcomeSummary: In small aortic annuli, self-expanding (Evolut) and balloon-expandable (SAPIEN 3) TAVR were clinically similar, but Evolut showed superior valve hemodynamics/less dysfunction.

entity.name: ACURATE IDE
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of death, stroke, or rehospitalization at 1 year
entity.outcomeSummary: ACURATE neo2 failed to meet noninferiority versus contemporary TAVR valves; results contributed to Boston Scientific discontinuing the platform.

curation.notes: Confirm publication/readout year and exact endpoint result.

entity.name: ALIGN-AR
entity.primaryEndpoint: Safety (mortality) and effectiveness vs performance goals
entity.outcomeSummary: JenaValve Trilogy met its endpoints in symptomatic severe aortic regurgitation, supporting the first dedicated transcatheter therapy for AR.

entity.name: EVEREST II
entity.primaryEndpoint: Freedom from death, MV surgery, and ≥3+ MR at 12 months
entity.outcomeSummary: MitraClip was less effective than surgery at reducing MR but safer, establishing TEER for selected patients.

entity.name: COAPT
entity.primaryEndpoint: Heart-failure hospitalizations at 24 months
entity.outcomeSummary: In secondary/functional MR with persistent HF symptoms, MitraClip reduced HF hospitalizations and mortality on top of GDMT.

entity.name: MITRA-FR
entity.primaryEndpoint: Death or unplanned HF hospitalization at 12 months
entity.outcomeSummary: In secondary MR, TEER showed no benefit over medical therapy — the COAPT/MITRA-FR contrast framed the proportionate vs disproportionate MR concept.

entity.name: CLASP IID
entity.primaryEndpoint: Safety and MR reduction; noninferiority vs MitraClip
entity.outcomeSummary: In degenerative MR, PASCAL was noninferior to MitraClip, supporting FDA approval.

entity.name: TRILUMINATE Pivotal
entity.primaryEndpoint: Hierarchical composite (death, surgery, HF hospitalization, QoL)
entity.outcomeSummary: TriClip reduced tricuspid regurgitation and improved quality of life, but without a significant reduction in death or HF hospitalization at 1 year.

entity.name: TRISCEND II
entity.primaryEndpoint: Hierarchical composite incl. symptoms/QoL and clinical events
entity.outcomeSummary: EVOQUE transcatheter tricuspid replacement improved symptoms, function, and quality of life versus optimized medical therapy.

entity.name: SUMMIT
entity.primaryEndpoint: Composite of mortality, HF hospitalization, and QoL
entity.outcomeSummary: US pivotal trial of Tendyne TMVR (incl. randomization vs TEER in suitable anatomy); informs the role of mitral replacement vs repair.

curation.notes: Confirm readout details/year as data mature.

entity.name: APOLLO
entity.primaryEndpoint: Safety and effectiveness of TMVR vs control
entity.outcomeSummary: US pivotal trial of the Medtronic Intrepid TMVR system; ongoing.

entity.name: ENCIRCLE
entity.primaryEndpoint: Safety and effectiveness of transseptal TMVR
entity.outcomeSummary: US pivotal trial of the Edwards SAPIEN M3 transseptal TMVR system; ongoing.

curation.notes: New York Valves 2026 (Jun 24-26): one-year ENCIRCLE-MAC Registry outcomes presented, supporting safety/effectiveness/QoL of SAPIEN M3 in mitral annular calcification. SAPIEN M3 reportedly received FDA approval in late 2025; human curator: verify and update dev-sapien-m3 regulatoryStatus accordingly.

entity.name: Harmony TPV Pivotal
entity.primaryEndpoint: Acceptable hemodynamic function and freedom from PV dysfunction
entity.outcomeSummary: Harmony TPV provided effective relief of pulmonary regurgitation in native/repaired RVOT, supporting FDA approval.
